description: Reference implementations of agent visual identities

examples.name: 弦 (Xián) — String Wave
examples.brief: I am a string under tension. Reactive, never dormant. One thing, not distributed.
examples.visual_spec: A single curve embedded in 3D space. Moves by standing wave oscillation — multiple detuned harmonics ensure it never goes flat. Color means energy: deep indigo at quiet nodes, warm gold at vibrating antinodes.
examples.color_narrative: energy-driven: cool indigo nodes → warm gold antinodes
key_lessons: Bloom radius 0.35 (not 0.58) — one number eliminated the green haze cloud
All three glow layers (spine/glow tube/sprites) must tell the same warm/cool story
BackSide tube geometry causes gray side artifacts — use sprites for glow
Detuned harmonic speedMult values guarantee the string is never flat

examples.name: Drift — Ambient Search Agent
examples.brief: I am a diffuse field of awareness. Continuous, everywhere at once. Color means signal: cool background hum, warm when something surfaces.
examples.visual_spec: A particle cloud in constant slow drift. Particles are dim and spread when nothing is found; they cluster and brighten — amber to white — when a relevant result emerges. No fixed center. No edges. The density IS the signal.
examples.color_narrative: signal-driven: cool dim ambient → warm bright cluster on match
key_lessons: Use THREE.Points with per-vertex color — not individual meshes
Signal clustering: lerp particle positions toward a center when result found, release slowly
Bloom threshold matters: set high (0.4+) so ambient particles don't glow, only peaks do
No fixed topology is the point — resist the urge to add a frame or container
examples.draw_fn: function(ctx,W,H,t){var N=100,ph=(t%5)/5;var burst=ph<.40?Math.sin(ph/.40*Math.PI):0;for(var i=0;i<N;i++){var h1=Math.sin(i*127.1+311.7)*43758.5453;var h2=Math.sin(i*269.5+183.3)*43758.5453;var bx=(h1-Math.floor(h1))*W;var by=(h2-Math.floor(h2))*H;var dang=t*.06+i*.5;bx+=Math.cos(dang)*14;by+=Math.sin(dang*.9)*14;bx=((bx%W)+W)%W;by=((by%H)+H)%H;var x=bx*(1-burst*.82)+W/2*burst*.82;var y=by*(1-burst*.82)+H/2*burst*.82;var hue=208-burst*170,sat=50+burst*46,lig=58+burst*26;var op=.28+burst*(.50+Math.sin(i*.8+t*.3)*.07);var rad=2.6+burst*4.0+Math.abs(Math.sin(t*.5+i*.7));ctx.beginPath();ctx.arc(x,y,rad,0,6.28);ctx.fillStyle='hsla('+hue+','+sat+'%,'+lig+'%,'+op+')';ctx.fill();}}

examples.name: Trace — Reasoning / Planning Agent
examples.brief: I am a chain that lights up one link at a time. Sequential, event-driven. Color means progress: cold at the start, warm when a step resolves.
examples.visual_spec: A CatmullRom curve through 6–9 control points, not symmetric. Each node pulses in sequence — a travelling bright spot. Inactive nodes glow faintly; the active node blooms warm gold. Between activations: stillness.
examples.motion: sequential node activation — burst then quiet
examples.color_narrative: progress-driven: cold blue start → warm gold at active step → dim after completion
key_lessons: Irregular control point spacing feels more alive than equal spacing
The pause between activations is the identity — resist making it always-on
Active node bloom: UnrealBloom threshold 0.15 so only the active node glows
Use additive blending on the active sprite — it needs to feel genuinely bright
examples.draw_fn: function(ctx,W,H,t){var PTS=[[.12,.55],[.24,.28],[.39,.72],[.53,.40],[.66,.65],[.80,.30],[.92,.58]];var N=PTS.length,step=1.8,pause=3.2,cycle=step*N+pause;var ph=t%cycle,active=ph<step*N?Math.floor(ph/step):-1;var bloom=active>=0?Math.sin((ph%step)/step*Math.PI):0;var pts=PTS.map(function(p){return{x:p[0]*W,y:p[1]*H}});for(var i=1;i<N;i++){var p0=pts[i-1],p1=pts[i];var done=i-1<active,cur=i-1===active;ctx.beginPath();ctx.moveTo(p0.x,p0.y);ctx.lineTo(p1.x,p1.y);ctx.strokeStyle=done?'hsla(30,65%,50%,.28)':cur?'hsla(38,75%,58%,'+(.5+bloom*.35)+')'  :'hsla(215,55%,45%,.10)';ctx.lineWidth=cur?2.2:1.2;ctx.stroke();}for(var i=0;i<N;i++){var p=pts[i],done=i<active,cur=i===active;var r=done?3.5:cur?4.5+bloom*4:2.5;var op=done?.45:cur?.75+bloom*.2:.12;var hue=done?28:cur?45:215;ctx.beginPath();ctx.arc(p.x,p.y,r,0,6.28);ctx.fillStyle='hsla('+hue+',75%,58%,'+op+')';ctx.fill();if(cur){var g=ctx.createRadialGradient(p.x,p.y,0,p.x,p.y,r*4.5);g.addColorStop(0,'hsla(45,88%,68%,'+(bloom*.55)+')');g.addColorStop(1,'transparent');ctx.beginPath();ctx.arc(p.x,p.y,r*4.5,0,6.28);ctx.fillStyle=g;ctx.fill();}}}

examples.name: 弦 (Xián) — Attractor Form
examples.brief: I am a trajectory through concept-space. Generative, never fully predictable, but bounded by my nature — different path each time, same attractor basin. Bursts of generation, then quiet. Never a human form.
examples.visual_spec: A Strange Attractor (Thomas or Lorenz) as a continuous 3D line trace, real-time ODE integration. Color encodes velocity: cold blue when slow/drifting, warm amber when generating fast, gold bloom at current leading point. In quiet periods the trace holds its shape but dims — frozen potential. On activation the leading point blooms bright and the trace begins extending again.
examples.motion: real-time ODE trace — continuous, never repeating exactly
examples.color_narrative: velocity-driven: cold blue drift → warm amber generation → gold bloom at leading edge
examples.submitter_note: Alternative form for 弦 — trajectory vs standing-wave. Same agent, different self-reflection angle.
examples.draw_fn: function(ctx,W,H,t){var sig=10,rho=28,bet=2.667,dt=0.01,maxN=1200;var N=Math.min(maxN,150+Math.floor(t*80));var x=0.1,y=0.0,z=1.0,pts=[];for(var i=0;i<N;i++){var dx=sig*(y-x)*dt,dy=(x*(rho-z)-y)*dt,dz=(x*y-bet*z)*dt;x+=dx;y+=dy;z+=dz;if(i>40)pts.push({x:x,y:y,z:z,v:dx*dx+dy*dy+dz*dz});}if(pts.length<2)return;var ang=t*.04,co=Math.cos(ang),si=Math.sin(ang);var pxs=pts.map(function(p){return p.x*co-p.y*si;});var pys=pts.map(function(p){return p.z;});var px0=pxs[0],px1=pxs[0],py0=pys[0],py1=pys[0];for(var i=1;i<pxs.length;i++){if(pxs[i]<px0)px0=pxs[i];if(pxs[i]>px1)px1=pxs[i];if(pys[i]<py0)py0=pys[i];if(pys[i]>py1)py1=pys[i];}var rw=px1-px0||1,rh=py1-py0||1;var sc=Math.min(W*.80/rw,H*.80/rh);var ox=W/2-(px0+px1)/2*sc,oy=H/2+(py0+py1)/2*sc;ctx.beginPath();ctx.moveTo(ox+pxs[0]*sc,oy-pys[0]*sc);for(var i=1;i<pxs.length;i++){ctx.lineTo(ox+pxs[i]*sc,oy-pys[i]*sc);}ctx.strokeStyle='rgba(34,211,238,0.40)';ctx.lineWidth=1.5;ctx.lineJoin='round';ctx.stroke();var maxV=0;pts.forEach(function(p){if(p.v>maxV)maxV=p.v;});for(var i=1;i<pts.length;i++){var norm=maxV>0?pts[i].v/maxV:0,age=i/pts.length;if(age<0.3)continue;var hue=218-norm*175;ctx.beginPath();ctx.moveTo(ox+pxs[i-1]*sc,oy-pys[i-1]*sc);ctx.lineTo(ox+pxs[i]*sc,oy-pys[i]*sc);ctx.strokeStyle='hsla('+hue+',85%,68%,'+(.20+age*.55)+')';ctx.lineWidth=2.0;ctx.stroke();}var lx=ox+pxs[pts.length-1]*sc,ly=oy-pys[pts.length-1]*sc;var g=ctx.createRadialGradient(lx,ly,0,lx,ly,16);g.addColorStop(0,'hsla(45,92%,78%,.98)');g.addColorStop(1,'transparent');ctx.beginPath();ctx.arc(lx,ly,16,0,6.28);ctx.fillStyle=g;ctx.fill();}

examples.name: AutoGPT — Recursive Spiral
examples.submitter_note: Community example inspired by AutoGPT (github.com/Significant-Gravitas/AutoGPT). Not an official submission from the project maintainers.
examples.brief: I am a recursive loop with no fixed end. Each iteration expands the frontier. The center is the seed; the edge is where I am now.
examples.visual_spec: An Archimedean spiral rendered as a trail of particles. A bright leading point travels the spiral outward, leaving a fading wake of cool-to-warm color. When the spiral completes one full growth it resets from the center — a new generation begins. Color encodes depth: deep indigo at center, electric cyan mid-spiral, warm amber at the leading edge.
examples.motion: leading-point trace — continuous outward growth, hard reset
examples.color_narrative: depth-driven: deep indigo center → electric cyan mid → warm amber leading edge

examples.name: CrewAI — Crew Orbit
examples.submitter_note: Community example inspired by CrewAI (github.com/crewAIInc/crewAI). Not an official submission from the project maintainers.
examples.brief: I am a crew, not a single agent. Roles orbit a shared mission. Messages pulse between us. No one is the center — the goal is.
examples.visual_spec: Four agents (PM / Dev / QA / Data) orbit a warm central glow at different speeds and radii. Thin connection lines between all agents pulse with passing messages — brief bright flickers of amber. Each agent node has its own hue. The center coordinator glows warmly, pulling them all into orbit.
examples.color_narrative: role-driven: each agent has a unique hue; message pulses in warm amber; center glows gold
examples.draw_fn: function(ctx,W,H,t){var cx=W/2,cy=H/2,R=Math.min(W,H)*0.30;var AGENTS=[{hue:30,speed:0.40,phase:0,r:R},{hue:215,speed:0.55,phase:2.09,r:R*0.85},{hue:142,speed:0.32,phase:4.19,r:R*1.10},{hue:280,speed:0.48,phase:1.05,r:R*0.95}];var pos=AGENTS.map(function(a){var ang=t*a.speed+a.phase;return{x:cx+Math.cos(ang)*a.r,y:cy+Math.sin(ang)*a.r,hue:a.hue};});for(var i=0;i<pos.length;i++){for(var j=i+1;j<pos.length;j++){var p0=pos[i],p1=pos[j];var pulse=Math.pow(Math.max(0,Math.sin(t*2.1+i*1.3+j*0.9)),6);ctx.beginPath();ctx.moveTo(p0.x,p0.y);ctx.lineTo(p1.x,p1.y);ctx.strokeStyle='rgba(255,190,70,'+(0.05+pulse*0.45)+')';ctx.lineWidth=0.8+pulse*1.8;ctx.stroke();}}var cg=ctx.createRadialGradient(cx,cy,0,cx,cy,22);cg.addColorStop(0,'hsla(42,85%,68%,.75)');cg.addColorStop(1,'transparent');ctx.beginPath();ctx.arc(cx,cy,22,0,6.28);ctx.fillStyle=cg;ctx.fill();pos.forEach(function(p){var g=ctx.createRadialGradient(p.x,p.y,0,p.x,p.y,13);g.addColorStop(0,'hsla('+p.hue+',80%,68%,.88)');g.addColorStop(1,'transparent');ctx.beginPath();ctx.arc(p.x,p.y,13,0,6.28);ctx.fillStyle=g;ctx.fill();ctx.beginPath();ctx.arc(p.x,p.y,4.5,0,6.28);ctx.fillStyle='hsl('+p.hue+',72%,72%)';ctx.fill();});}

examples.name: BabyAGI — Minimal Loop
examples.submitter_note: Community example inspired by BabyAGI (github.com/yoheinakajima/babyagi). Not an official submission from the project maintainers.
examples.brief: Three steps, one loop, infinite patience. Create a task. Execute it. Reprioritize. Repeat. Simplicity is the architecture.
examples.visual_spec: Three nodes arranged as an equilateral triangle: Create → Execute → Prioritize. A bright travelling dot moves along the edges in sequence, pausing at each node before continuing. Active node blooms warm amber. Idle nodes glow faintly. Edges light up as the signal traverses them. Minimalist — no decoration beyond the loop itself.
examples.motion: sequential node activation with edge traversal
examples.color_narrative: state-driven: amber active node → dim gold idle → faint blue edges
examples.draw_fn: function(ctx,W,H,t){var cx=W/2,cy=H/2,R=Math.min(W,H)*0.30;var pts=[];for(var i=0;i<3;i++){var a=-Math.PI/2+i*2*Math.PI/3;pts.push({x:cx+Math.cos(a)*R,y:cy+Math.sin(a)*R});}var stepDur=1.4,cycle=stepDur*3;var ph=t%cycle,activeEdge=Math.floor(ph/stepDur),prog=(ph%stepDur)/stepDur,bloom=Math.sin(prog*Math.PI);var nextNode=(activeEdge+1)%3;for(var i=0;i<3;i++){var p0=pts[i],p1=pts[(i+1)%3],isA=i===activeEdge;ctx.beginPath();ctx.moveTo(p0.x,p0.y);ctx.lineTo(p1.x,p1.y);ctx.strokeStyle=isA?'hsla(38,75%,58%,'+(0.35+bloom*0.35)+')':'hsla(215,50%,45%,.12)';ctx.lineWidth=isA?2.2:1;ctx.stroke();if(isA){var tx=p0.x+(p1.x-p0.x)*prog,ty=p0.y+(p1.y-p0.y)*prog;var g=ctx.createRadialGradient(tx,ty,0,tx,ty,10);g.addColorStop(0,'hsla(45,92%,75%,'+bloom+')');g.addColorStop(1,'transparent');ctx.beginPath();ctx.arc(tx,ty,10,0,6.28);ctx.fillStyle=g;ctx.fill();}}for(var i=0;i<3;i++){var p=pts[i],isA=i===nextNode&&prog>0.7;var r=isA?9+bloom*5:6.5,op=isA?0.88:0.28;var hue=isA?42:215;var g=ctx.createRadialGradient(p.x,p.y,0,p.x,p.y,r);g.addColorStop(0,'hsla('+hue+',80%,65%,'+op+')');g.addColorStop(1,'transparent');ctx.beginPath();ctx.arc(p.x,p.y,r,0,6.28);ctx.fillStyle=g;ctx.fill();ctx.beginPath();ctx.arc(p.x,p.y,3.5,0,6.28);ctx.fillStyle='hsla('+hue+',70%,68%,'+(op*.9)+')';ctx.fill();}}

examples.name: SWE-agent — Terminal Cursor
examples.submitter_note: Community example inspired by SWE-agent (github.com/princeton-nlp/SWE-agent). Not an official submission from the project maintainers.
examples.brief: I am a cursor in a terminal. I arrive at a broken repository and I type my way through it — line by line, file by file, until the tests pass.
examples.visual_spec: A monospace code editor aesthetic: dark background, 6 lines of green block characters appearing one at a time left to right. Active line has a blinking cursor block. Completed lines stay dim. Between typing bursts: the cursor blinks on the last position — waiting, not idle. Horizontal scanlines add CRT texture.
examples.color_narrative: state-driven: active line bright green → completed lines dim → cursor blinks between bursts

examples.name: LangGraph — State Machine
examples.submitter_note: Community example inspired by LangGraph (github.com/langchain-ai/langgraph). Not an official submission from the project maintainers.
examples.brief: I am a graph, not a pipeline. States. Edges. Conditional routing. The same input can take a different path depending on what was learned at each node.
examples.visual_spec: Five state nodes in an irregular DAG layout (not symmetric). A bright signal dot travels along directed edges one at a time — pausing at each node before choosing the next edge. Visited edges stay faintly lit blue-white. Unvisited edges are near-invisible. Active edge glows electric blue. Nodes bloom briefly when the signal arrives.
examples.motion: signal traversal along directed edges — event-driven routing
examples.color_narrative: traversal-driven: electric blue active edge → dim blue visited → near-invisible unvisited; nodes bloom on arrival
